finding: An agent's personal financial interest determined which abstract principle (among multiple available) they invoked to justify their self-serving choices; the principle chosen varied by context but consistently maximized the agent's payoff.

The ordinary concept of weakness of will is better captured as a prototype or cluster concept influenced by multiple factors, rather than a single definition based on acting contrary to one's judgments.
Preview benefit from a word two positions ahead occurred less reliably in reading when the intermediate word was not a high-frequency function word; foveal load modulates the perceptual span.

The burden of an infectious disease correlates with erosion of non-disease-related health services; regions with the highest disease prevalence show the largest declines in care for unrelated conditions.

Partnership formation improves well-being; well-being gains from marriage exceed those from cohabitation; effects vary by demographic group and partnership type, though same-sex effects are equivalent within gender.

An action-versus-inaction framing (commission versus omission) altered moral judgment of identical consequences; inactions were judged less immoral and worse as decisions, mediated by judgments about causation.

The number of product units displayed on a package anchored consumers' quantity perception and subsequent consumption; this effect persists despite verbally provided quantity information but is moderated by visual processing depth.

An intensive, teacher-led early reading intervention combining letter knowledge, phonetic decoding, and connected text reading improved reading and spelling in at-risk students; adaptive features on a computer component added no significant benefit.

Pairing of neutral tones with a participant's own name versus other names produced a larger ERP response to the person's own name; classical conditioning of ERP responses to the paired tones occurred inconsistently across experiments, even when the names were not consciously perceived.

Visual information about group dynamics dominated rapid judgments of ensemble performance despite sound being the stated standard; observers selected correct high-performers from silent video but not from audio alone.

Government perceived as impartial toward ethnic groups associates with sustained economic growth; the relationship holds independently of the content of policies, as long as implementation is perceived as fair.

A manufacturing strategy emphasizing flexibility through process simplicity and procedural discipline proved valid and applicable across multiple European countries; the approach demonstrated positive empirical support.

Repeated exposure to an object (checking or use) reduced memory accuracy, confidence, vividness, and detail equally; the effect occurred without action and persisted after delay, suggesting the mechanism is experiential rather than behavioral.
Negative valence of an agent's action (harm, blameworthiness, norm violation) increased attribution of knowledge in Gettier cases; the effect combines epistemological and evaluative judgments.
Political polarization correlates with smaller government in democracies but not in non-democracies; the relationship is independent of confounders such as wealth and inequality.

Crime victimization increased political participation across multiple regions and crime types; the effect is as large as five to ten years of additional education, though victimization sometimes associates with support for authoritarian responses.

The social prevalence of emancipative values increased protest participation both directly (elevator effect) and by amplifying the impact of individuals' own values (amplifier effect); ecological effects amplified individual effects.

A police killing in the United States negatively affected racial attitudes toward law enforcement among members of the same racial group in another country; the effect was concentrated within that racial group.

Cigarette smoking was as strong an independent predictor of psychotic-like experiences as cannabis use; the cannabis-psychosis association was confounded by smoking when both were included in the model.

Perceived threat from immigration reduced trust in national institutions and politicians; this effect persists when controlling for other predictors and is mediated by a country's postwar immigration history.
